pairs.question: What notable positions has Dardan Selimaj held within the Kosovo Philharmonic?
pairs.answer: Dardan Selimaj has been a member of the choir of the Kosovo Philharmonic since 2003 and is currently the manager of the Kosovo Philharmonic.

pairs.question: Which festivals did Dardan Selimaj manage between 2006 and 2007?
pairs.answer: Between 2006 and 2007, Dardan Selimaj managed the Prishtina Jazz Festival.

pairs.question: What was the name of Dardan Selimaj's first documentary film, and when was it premiered?
pairs.answer: Dardan Selimaj's first documentary film was titled 'There is light inside,' co-directed with Rea Surroi. It premiered in 2014 at Dokufest in Prizren, Kosovo.

pairs.question: How did Dardan Selimaj contribute to the International Festival of Young Musicians DAM?
pairs.answer: Dardan Selimaj co-founded the International Festival of Young Musicians DAM in 2006 alongside Yllka Rexhepi and has served as the head of the festival since its inception. He also organizes concerts in Kosovo as part of his role.

pairs.question: Provide an example of Dardan Selimaj's work as a journalist and TV host.
pairs.answer: Dardan Selimaj started his journalism career by writing for magazines and hosting a live show on a local radio station in Prishtina. Since 2005, he has hosted a daily TV show on Kohavision (KTV), one of Kosovo's three national broadcasters.

pairs.question: What are some international locations where Dardan Selimaj has performed with the Kosovo Philharmonic choir?
pairs.answer: Dardan Selimaj has performed with the choir of the Kosovo Philharmonic in Croatia, Albania, and North Macedonia.

pairs.question: What is one of Dardan Selimaj's recent ventures outside of his established career in journalism and music?
pairs.answer: Lately, Dardan Selimaj has ventured into documentary filmmaking, as seen with his 2014 documentary 'There is light inside' and his involvement in screening events and festivals internationally.
